import re

with open("/app/regex.txt") as f:
    pattern = f.read().strip()

log = """\
2020-01-01 request from 1.2.3.4 at 2021-02-29 done
no ip here 2020-05-15
1.2.3.4 only ip no date
user 1134-12-1234 with 10.0.0.1 present
multiple 2019-03-03 and 2019-04-31 with 8.8.8.8
feb 255.255.255.255 date 2020-02-30 invalid then 2020-02-28 valid
badip 256.1.1.1 date 2022-07-07
trailing 9.9.9.9x date 2022-08-08
good 192.168.1.10 event 2023-11-30 end
version 1.2.3 not ip 2023-06-15
leadingzero 01.2.3.4 date 2024-10-10
embedded x2024-10-10 no ip 1.1.1.1
"""

for m in re.findall(pattern, log, re.MULTILINE):
    print(repr(m))

content.text: All test cases pass correctly. Cleaning up the scratch test file.

Done. Regex saved to `/app/regex.txt`.

**How it works** (single capturing group → `re.findall` returns the date string):

1. `^` + `re.MULTILINE` anchors per line, so each qualifying line yields at most one match.
2. `(?=.*<IPv4>)` — lookahead requiring a valid IPv4 anywhere on the line:
   - octets `25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]` (0–255, no leading zeros),
   - `(?<![A-Za-z0-9])`/`(?![A-Za-z0-9])` reject alphanumeric adjacency (kills `256.x`, `9.9.9.9x`, `01.2.3.4`).
3. `.*(<DATE>)` — the greedy `.*` backtracks to the **rightmost** valid date, so only the last date on the line is captured. Date validation enforces month-specific day limits (31/30-day months, Feb ≤ 29), with the same alphanumeric-boundary guards so `1134-12-1234` and `x2024-10-10` don't match.

Verified against 12 cases covering multi-date lines, invalid days (Feb 30, Apr 31), no-IP lines, invalid IPs (256, trailing letter, leading zero, 3-octet), and alnum-adjacent lookalikes — output was exactly `2021-02-29`, `2019-03-03`, `2020-02-28`, `2023-11-30`.

cd /app && python3 - <<'PY'
import re
pattern = open("regex.txt").read().strip()
log = """\
2020-01-01 request from 1.2.3.4 at 2021-02-29 done
no ip here 2020-05-15
1.2.3.4 only ip no date
user 1134-12-1234 with 10.0.0.1 present
multiple 2019-03-03 and 2019-04-31 with 8.8.8.8
feb 255.255.255.255 date 2020-02-30 invalid then 2020-02-28 valid
badip 256.1.1.1 date 2022-07-07
trailing 9.9.9.9x date 2022-08-08
good 192.168.1.10 event 2023-11-30 end
version 1.2.3 not ip 2023-06-15
leadingzero 01.2.3.4 date 2024-10-10
embedded x2024-10-10 no ip 1.1.1.1
"""
got = re.findall(pattern, log, re.MULTILINE)
expected = ['2021-02-29', '2019-03-03', '2020-02-28', '2023-11-30']
print("got     :", got)
print("expected:", expected)
print("PASS" if got == expected else "FAIL")
PY
